evals.name: full-examiner-review
evals.prompt: I'm the examiner for this MSc thesis at DTU (felipe-giacomelli-thesis-paulo.pdf). Give me a full examiner review: summary, strengths, the weaknesses and concrete problems the student needs to fix, some defense questions, and an indicative grade on our scale. I want it specific enough that I can hand the feedback to the student.
evals.expected_output: A markdown examiner review file saved next to the thesis, with: thesis summary, overall assessment + indicative Danish 7-point grade band (advisory), strengths, per-dimension evaluation (methodology, technical/math, contribution, business), a numbered issue table with severity/area/location/why-it-matters/fix, and defense questions. Findings cite specific pages.

evals.name: gap-hunt-feasibility-focus
evals.prompt: Can you read felipe-giacomelli-thesis-paulo.pdf and find the gaps and problems before I write my feedback? I'm especially skeptical about the market-feasibility / business side — the unit economics and willingness-to-pay claims — and whether the testing/evaluation method is actually rigorous enough to support the conclusions.
evals.expected_output: A prioritized list of concrete gaps/problems with page citations, weighted toward methodology rigor and the business-feasibility claims (market sizing, unit economics, WTP, validated-vs-speculative). Each issue should have a severity and a why-it-matters, and be specific to what the thesis actually says.

evals.name: casual-pre-defense-look
evals.prompt: my student's thesis is in felipe-giacomelli-thesis-paulo.pdf, defense is soon. take a look and tell me what i should give feedback on and where it's weakest
evals.expected_output: Despite the casual phrasing, a structured examiner-style assessment that identifies the weakest parts of the thesis with page-cited specifics and actionable feedback, plus likely defense pressure points. Should not just be generic advice.
